subject: Security alert: New sign-in from Chrome on Windows
body:
Hi there,

We noticed a new sign-in to your GitHub account from:

Device: Chrome 125.0 on Windows 11
Location: Dhaka, Bangladesh
IP: 103.48.16.***
Time: June 11, 2026 at 3:42 PM UTC

If this was you, you can safely ignore this email. If you don't recognize this activity, please secure your account immediately.

Thanks,
The GitHub Team

subject: [WARNING] API latency increased to 2.3s (threshold: 500ms)
body:
⚠️ ALERT TRIGGERED

Metric: api.request.latency.p95
Current value: 2.3s
Threshold: 500ms
Duration: 15 minutes

Affected endpoints:
- /api/v2/users (3.1s)
- /api/v2/orders (2.8s)
- /api/v2/products (1.9s)

Possible causes:
- Database connection pool exhaustion
- Increased traffic volume
- Memory pressure on app servers

View dashboard: https://app.datadoghq.com/dashboard/abc123

This alert will auto-resolve when latency returns to normal.

subject: Re: Quarterly business review - scheduling conflict
body:
Hi Team,

I need to reschedule our QBR meeting originally set for June 20th. Our CEO just booked an all-hands meeting that day.

Can we move it to either:
- June 22nd at 2 PM EST
- June 24th at 10 AM EST
- June 25th at 3 PM EST

Please let me know what works for your team. Apologies for the inconvenience.

Best regards,
Sarah Johnson
Chief Technology Officer
Enterprise Client Inc.

subject: DDoS attack mitigated: 45M requests blocked
body:
SECURITY INCIDENT REPORT

Domain: api.yourdomain.com
Attack type: Layer 7 HTTP Flood
Duration: 2 hours 34 minutes
Peak volume: 180,000 req/sec
Total blocked: 45.2M requests

Attack characteristics:
- Source: Botnet (primarily China, Russia, Brazil)
- Target: /api/v2/auth endpoint
- Method: GET requests with random query parameters
- User-Agent rotation: 500+ unique strings

Mitigation actions:
✓ Rate limiting applied
✓ Challenge page enabled for suspicious IPs
✓ WAF rules updated

Service impact: None (100% availability maintained)

View detailed analytics: https://dash.cloudflare.com/security

subject: Partnership proposal: Integration opportunity
body:
Hi there,

I'm Michael Chen, VP of Partnerships at Partner Company. We've been following your platform's growth and see strong synergy between our products.

We'd like to explore a potential integration partnership that could:
- Expand your product capabilities
- Provide mutual customers with seamless workflow
- Create co-marketing opportunities

Would you be open to a 30-minute call next week to discuss? I'm available:
- Tuesday 2-4 PM EST
- Wednesday 10 AM-12 PM EST
- Thursday 3-5 PM EST

Looking forward to connecting!

Best,
Michael Chen
VP of Partnerships
Partner Company
michael.chen@partner-company.com

subject: AWS Cost Anomaly Detection alert
body:
AWS Cost Anomaly Detection has identified unusual spending:

Account: 1234-5678-9012
Service: Amazon EC2
Anomaly detected: June 10, 2026
Expected cost: $450
Actual cost: $1,247
Variance: +177% ($797 over expected)

Root cause analysis:
- 3 new t3.2xlarge instances launched in us-east-1
- Running for 72+ hours without termination
- No associated Auto Scaling group

Recommended actions:
1. Review instance necessity
2. Enable Auto Scaling if needed
3. Set up billing alerts
4. Consider Reserved Instances for long-term workloads

View details: https://console.aws.amazon.com/costmanagement

subject: Apdex score dropped to 0.62 (threshold: 0.80)
body:
APPLICATION PERFORMANCE ALERT

Application: Production Web App
Metric: Apdex Score
Current: 0.62 (Poor)
Threshold: 0.80 (Good)
Duration: 45 minutes

Impact analysis:
- Slow transactions: 34% (was 8%)
- Error rate: 2.3% (was 0.4%)
- Throughput: 1,247 rpm (normal)

Slowest transactions:
1. GET /api/users/profile - 4.2s (p95)
2. POST /api/orders - 3.8s (p95)
3. GET /api/products - 2.9s (p95)

Possible causes:
- Database query performance degradation
- External API latency increase
- Memory pressure on app servers

Investigate: https://one.newrelic.com/dashboards

subject: FURIOUS: 3rd outage this month - demanding compensation
body:
To Whom It May Concern,

This is ABSOLUTELY UNACCEPTABLE! For the THIRD TIME this month, your service has been down during our critical business hours.

Outage details:
- June 3: 2 hours (9-11 AM)
- June 8: 4 hours (2-6 PM)
- June 11: 3 hours (10 AM-1 PM)

Total downtime: 9 hours this month
SLA guarantee: 99.9% uptime (max 43 minutes/month)

Financial impact:
- Lost revenue: ~$15,000
- Customer complaints: 47
- Team productivity loss: 120 hours

We demand:
1. Full refund for June ($2,400)
2. Service credits for next 3 months
3. Written explanation of root cause
4. Concrete plan to prevent future outages

If this is not resolved within 24 hours, we will:
- Terminate our contract immediately
- Demand damages for breach of SLA
- Post negative reviews on G2, Capterra, Trustpilot
- Report to consumer protection agencies

I expect your response by 5 PM tomorrow.

Jennifer Williams
CTO, Angry Customer Corp
jennifer.williams@angry-customer.com
(555) 123-4567

subject: Dyno quota warning: 92% of monthly hours used
body:
Heroku Dyno Quota Alert

Account: your-app-name
Plan: Hobby ($7/month)
Billing period: June 1-30, 2026

Dyno hours used: 662 / 720 (92%)
Remaining: 58 hours
Estimated exhaustion: June 28, 2026

Current dynos:
- web.1: Running (24/7)
- worker.1: Running (24/7)

When quota is exceeded:
- Dynos will sleep
- App will become unresponsive
- Wake-up time: 30-60 seconds

Options:
1. Scale down dynos to save hours
2. Upgrade to Standard plan ($25/month, unlimited hours)
3. Add more dyno hours ($0.05/hour)

Manage dynos: https://dashboard.heroku.com/apps/your-app

Questions? support@heroku.com
